subject: Geography
title: The Oldest Known Map
description: Learn The Oldest Known Map in one minute.

hook.text: A 14,000-year-old cave painting in Spain may be the oldest map ever found.

buildup.text: It appears to show hills, rivers, and paths used by hunters to track game.

discovery.text: Babylonians carved the first confirmed maps into clay tablets around 2300 BCE.

twist.text: These early maps weren't about geography—they tracked land ownership and tax borders.

climax.text: From the start, maps were tools of power, showing who owned what.

punchline.text: The first maps weren't about exploration. They were about control.

quiz.question: What were the earliest confirmed maps used for?
